item.title: Baht Drops to One-Year Low on Expectations of Rate Gap With US
item.description: The baht falls to a one-year low on expectations of a wider interest rate gap between the US and Thailand as domestic demand comes under pressure from multiple headwinds.

item.title: Singapore’s Core Inflation Steady at 1.4% Despite Oil Crunch
item.description: Singapore’s core inflation rate was unchanged last month, even as the Middle East conflict had pushed up global prices of energy and fertilizer.

item.title: GM-Backed Self-Driving Firm Momenta Said to Near Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Momenta Global Ltd. is set to start gauging investor interest for an initial public offering in Hong Kong as early as this week, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Global Funds Snap Indian Stock Selling Streak as Oil Shock Ebbs
item.description: Foreign investors turned buyers of Indian equities for the first time in two months as easing Middle East tensions and lower oil prices revive appetite for the market.

item.title: Tencent in Talks to Offload Marvelous and Other Global Game Bets
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. is negotiating exits from several game studio investments in Japan, including Tokyo-traded Marvelous Inc., as part of a reassessment of the company’s global portfolio, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Iran Rushes to Woo Asia’s Largest Oil Importers After US Waiver
item.description: Iran is racing to court some of Asia’s largest oil buyers as a 60-day US sanctions waiver takes effect, a temporary lifeline that should allow Tehran to resume exports and begin clearing a backlog of cargoes on the water, even as peace talks continue.

item.title: Masayoshi Son Dismisses Musk’s Idea for Orbital Data Centers
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. founder Masayoshi Son said there’s little merit to building data centers in space, as championed by Elon Musk, predicting that the AI race will be clinched by compute on Earth.

item.title: Deutsche Bank Cuts Gold Forecasts up to 22% as Bulls Temper View
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG reduced its gold-price forecasts by as much as 22%, as investors become more wary about the outlook for US monetary policy and investment demand for the precious metal dries up.

item.title: Greeks Get Priced Out of Summer Even After Economic Recovery
item.description: Life isn’t what it used to be as a squeeze on household incomes increasingly makes long beach vacations a relic of a more prosperous past.

item.title: Greenspan’s Legacy Is as Convoluted as His Words
item.description: His reputation no longer lies in post-crisis tatters, but should it inspire Trump 2.0?

item.title: Hungary Likely to Cut Rates as Forint Gains and Inflation Slows
item.description: Hungary’s central bank is likely to lower borrowing costs for the first time in months as slowing inflation and a stronger currency give policymakers room to diverge from global peers starting monetary tightening.

item.title: Japan’s Five-Year Bond Sale Draws Soft Demand on Rate Hike Fears
item.description: Japan’s five-year government bond auction Tuesday saw lower demand than the 12-month average, as yen weakness added to expectations that the Bank of Japan may need to raise rates faster.

item.title: India to Share Rocket Tech to Hasten Development of Space Firms
item.description: India said it plans to share its technology involving the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle, the country’s most reliable rocket, to help speed up development of the local space industry.

item.title: CR New Energy’s Record IPO Gets Strong Retail Demand in Shenzhen
item.description: China Resources New Energy Holdings Ltd. drew strong demand from investors as it opened books for what is set to be the largest-ever initial public offering on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange, underscoring the appetite for new listings even beyond the artificial intelligence supply chain.

item.title: Aluminum Falls as Peace Talk Progress Points to Returning Supply
item.description: Aluminum touched a three-month low as progress in Middle East peace talks fueled expectations of a return of supplies from the key metal-producing region.

item.title: SK Hynix ETF’s Assets Soar to $17 Billion, Biggest in Hong Kong
item.description: CSOP Asset Management Ltd.’s leveraged exchange traded product tied to SK Hynix Inc. has overtaken the Tracker Fund of Hong Kong to become the city’s largest ETF, highlighting investors’ voracious appetite for risky bets on the booming semiconductor sector.

item.title: Seasonality, Contrarian Buying May Lift Battered Indian IT Shares
item.description: The average return in the July-December period has been 16%.

item.title: Circle K Owner’s Fuel Profits Surge Despite Soft Demand in US, Europe
item.description: Gas station and convenience store operator Alimentation Couche-Tard Inc. saw big gains in fuel margins after the war in the Middle East caused prices to spike. The company easily beat analysts’ estimates for sales and earnings.

item.title: CATL Debuts Battery Storage System Using New Sodium Technology
item.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. has launched a new sodium battery storage system, and expects to start delivering it to China in September.

item.title: Korean Stocks Fall More Than 4% From Record High on Tech Selloff
item.description: South Korean stocks tumbled from a record high as investors sold off chip heavyweights on renewed concern that the rally has become overstretched.

item.title: China Pursues Austerity With First Fiscal Gap Cutback Since 2023
item.description: China narrowed its cumulative fiscal deficit for the first time in more than two years, pressing ahead with austerity despite muted domestic demand and slowing economic growth.

item.title: Amazon Ordered to Collectively Bargain at San Francisco Warehouse
item.description: A US labor board judge ruled that Amazon.com Inc. must collectively bargain with California warehouse workers, teeing up a legal battle that could end with President Donald Trump’s appointees reversing a major union-friendly precedent.

item.title: India’s Inflation Is Still Anchored Despite May Pickup, RBI Says
item.description: Indian inflation remains anchored below the central bank’s target despite the recent energy shock, as pass-through to domestic prices has been limited, the Reserve Bank said in a monthly bulletin.

item.title: US-Japan More Aligned on FX, Katayama Says After Bessent Talks
item.description: Japan’s Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said she had talks over the phone with US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, a move that only gave the yen a short-lived boost as it hovers near the weakest level in four decades.

item.title: ‘A Lot of Leeway’: SpaceX’s High-Grade Debt Brings Out Skeptics
item.description: When Moody’s Ratings first evaluated Nvidia Corp. almost a decade ago, it settled on a Baa1 investment-grade rating, taking comfort in its relatively light debt load and more than $1 billion of free cash flow after 16 years as a public company.

item.title: IFM Investors Nears 50% Stake in Atlas Arteria as Deadline Looms
item.description: IFM Investors is nearing a 50% holding in Australia-listed toll road operator Atlas Arteria Ltd., ahead of the Thursday night deadline on its takeover bid.

item.title: ‘FOMO Really Got Me’: Taiwanese Go Deep Into Debt to Amp 100% Stock Rally
item.description: Taiwan's locals are borrowing so much to invest in the TSMC-fueled AI stock frenzy it's raising fears of a bubble.

item.title: India’s Biggest Equity Fund Makes Contrarian Bet on IT Stocks
item.description: India’s largest actively managed equity fund is betting against investor fears over artificial intelligence’s impact on the outsourcing industry as it snaps up the nation’s beaten-down IT stocks.

item.title: CFTC Seeks Input on 24/7 Futures, Energy Perpetuals Trades
item.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission asked for public input on both perpetual contracts and 24/7 trading for traditional energy derivatives, after industry heavyweights raised concerns about offshore platforms and excessive levels of risk.

item.title: China Links Offshore, Onshore Yuan in Tweak Toward Broader Use
item.description: China is taking another step in its longstanding push to build a more global currency, bringing its domestic and offshore yuan markets a little closer together.
